flags: Concentration: SPY is 30% of the book

moves.note: 2x space, small size, worst drawdown ratio
Cut semi concentration, free margin safely
Top scanner score, tight stop, cyber diversifies
Best R ratio, only enter on confirmed trigger
Semi exposure via fundamentals not leverage
Income drag, trim to fund higher-conviction adds

plan: RISK FIRST: Exit SOXL and SPAL immediately — together they represent 8% of book but carry the worst loss-to-size ratios on the sheet, and 3x/2x levered names in a margin account stack risk on top of risk you already carry.

Trim SMH from 13% to 7% and QQQI from 18% to 12% — this frees roughly 12 points of book, reduces your semiconductor concentration from 19% toward ~12%, and lowers the true leverage closer to ~1.05x, pulling the bad-month estimate down meaningfully.

Deploy proceeds in two steps: initiate PANW at ~5% of book (highest scanner conviction at 9/12, entry 351.34–351.69, hard stop 350.62) and TSM at ~4% (semi exposure backed by fundamentals rather than a leverage wrapper, entry 443.34–443.79, stop 442.47).

Place ARM on conditional Watch at 4% — the R of 9.46 is the best on the sheet, but the score is 7/12 and the entry range is wide, so only add if price confirms inside 336.80–337.13 with your standard trigger discipline.
After all moves, total adds (~9%) are covered by total trims + exits (~14%), leaving a small cash/margin buffer — this keeps you from adding gross exposure before you have cut the riskiest names.
read: A 2.5%/month target on a book that is ~90% tech/chip-concentrated on margin is possible in strong trending months but the math works against consistency — your own data shows a bad month can erase three or four winning months in a single drawdown of ~18%, and levered ETFs like SOXL structurally decay over time.
